statement.en: “This is how I look at every place of my ministry: a monk has his native land everywhere...” Letters from metropolitan Nikandr (Fenomenov) to metropolitan Flavian (Gorodetsky)
statement.summary: The publication introduces into the scientific circulation the letters from the future well-known church hierarch of the early twentieth century, Metropolitan Nikander (Fenomenov), the closest assistant of His Holiness Patriarch Tikhon, to another outstanding archpastor of the late 19th and early 20th centuries, a permanent member of the Holy Synod and the closest friend of Chief Prosecutor K. P. Pobedonostsev, Metropolitan Flavian (Gorodetsky) of Kiev. The letters relate to different periods of the future bishop's life - the starting point is determined by the time of his service in Georgia (1900-1905), where, in fact, he met His Grace the addressee. The last letter is dated as January of 1915 and was written, thus, ten months before the death of Metropolitan Flavian, while the addressee
